tasks.description: Build the per-slice hard gate: a single runnable command (`make verify-slice SLICE=<key>`, or an equivalent `bin/verify-slice`) that composes a slice's behavioral pytest selection + the project static gate (ruff, ruff format --check, mypy ., make test-js) + context-validate (jq-on-.output envelope, CTX-13) into ONE exit code, partitioned two-class per DEC-58/DEC-53: only the BLOCKING set (the slice's behavioral tests + this-slice-introduced soundness) determines the exit code; cosmetic/style, language-register WARNs, and pre-existing/unrelated/flaky failures are NON-BLOCKING and reported as structured warnings, never changing a passing exit code. acceptance_criteria: Running the slice gate command for a given slice exits 0 if and only if every BLOCKING check passes: the slice's behavioral tests, and the soundness (types, project test suite) that the slice's own code is responsible for.

The command's exit code is the sole pass/fail verdict — no consumer needs to read prose to decide pass or fail.
The pre-existing whole-project gate (the `test` target and the commit hook's checks) remains green and is not weakened by adding this gate.

tasks.description: Accumulation seam sub-slice 2 (simple-formset fold): a pure server-side function (planner/services.py, sibling to fold_form_step_prefill) folding a parsed formset-step AssistResult.prefill -- a list[dict] of per-row field values -- into the flat draft_state as {prefix}-{i}-{field} keys, where prefix == the spec's target_step (B1 criteria / D1 milestones / F1 steps / C1 feedback / A2 standards). tasks.description: Accumulation seam sub-slice 3b (G1 review-loop fold): a G1-specific router in planner/services.py folding the propose-review-loop prefill (one object: communications, review_events with nested inputs, revision_rules) into draft_state. Routes the 3 flat sections through fold_formset_prefill with the correct prefixes -- communications->'review' (NAME MAPPING; prefill key 'communications' != prefix 'review'), review_events->'review_events' (scalars only, with the 'inputs' list stripped), revision_rules->'revision_rules'. NEW logic for the nested inputs: per review_event i, write review_events-{i}-input-{j}-channel_index (prefill key 'inputs' PLURAL -> flat token 'input' SINGULAR), REPLACE-scoped ^review_events-\d+-input-\d+-[^-]+$ (does not touch event scalars or nested management forms). Index refs (channel_index/trigger_index/milestone_index) stored as integers, resolved at save. Carries forward the A3 round-2 lessons: ATOMIC on guard failure (validate all sections + nested shapes before mutating) + raise on unexpected prefill keys. Addresses FEAT-010 / STORY-013; harness SLICE=fold_g1.
acceptance_criteria: Folding the G1 prefill sets communications as review-{i}-{field}, review_events scalars as review_events-{i}-{field} (the 'inputs' list NOT written as a scalar field), revision_rules as revision_rules-{i}-{field}, and each review_event's nested inputs as review_events-{i}-input-{j}-channel_index.

The prefill key 'communications' maps to the 'review' prefix; the nested prefill key 'inputs' (plural) maps to the flat token 'input' (singular); index references (channel_index, trigger_index, milestone_index) are stored as integers unchanged (not resolved at fold time).

REPLACE holds per section (re-folding clears that section's prior direct rows incl. stale higher-index) and the nested-input REPLACE clears review_events-{i}-input-{j}-* for rewritten events without clobbering the event's scalar fields or nested management-form keys; the 'review' (communications) REPLACE does not touch review_events/revision_rules keys.
The fold is ATOMIC on guard failure: a malformed section or nested-input shape leaves draft_state entirely untouched; an unexpected top-level prefill key raises a clear domain error.

The folded draft_state round-trips through PlanDraftState and is consumable: _draft_state_row_count returns 'review'/'review_events'/'revision_rules' counts and the nested review_events-{i}-input-{j}-channel_index keys read back with their integer values.
Pure, imports no retired-workshop code; the whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates; nothing pre-existing breaks.

tasks.description: Accumulation seam sub-slice 4 (per-step-children fold): a generic server-side function fold_per_step_child_prefill in planner/services.py folding the per-step-child prefills (F2 assignment, F3 timeline, F4 resource+substep, F5 evidence, US-LLM-27 responsibility+position_responsibility) -- each list[{step_index, ...}] -- into draft_state under steps-{step_index}-{segment}-{k}-{field}. Handles single-row segments (F2/F3 -> row 0 from the row's direct fields) and multi-row segments (F4/F5/US-LLM-27 -> {k} rows from a named nested list field); a spec with two segments (F4 resource+substep, US-LLM-27 responsibility+position_responsibility) calls it once per segment. step_index + any index/pk refs stored as integers/strings as-is (resolved at save). REPLACE scoped per (step_index, segment): ^steps-{i}-{segment}-\d+-[^-]+$ (collision-safe incl. responsibility vs position_responsibility). Atomic pre-validation + unexpected-key guard per the A3/G1 lessons. Reuses the review_loop nested-fold precedent. Addresses FEAT-010 / STORY-013; harness SLICE=fold_stepchild.
acceptance_criteria: For each prefill row, the fold writes that row's step_index's child-segment keys: a single-row segment -> steps-{step_index}-{segment}-0-{field} from the row's direct fields; a multi-row segment -> steps-{step_index}-{segment}-{k}-{field} from the named nested list (one {k} per list element).
step_index and any pk/index reference fields are stored as integers/strings unchanged (not resolved at fold time).

REPLACE per (step_index, segment): re-folding a step's segment clears its prior steps-{step_index}-{segment}-{k}-* rows (incl. stale higher-index) without clobbering the step's own scalar fields, OTHER segments under the same step, the segment's nested management-form keys, or other steps.
Folding segment 'responsibility' does not touch 'position_responsibility' keys and vice versa (the prefix-overlap is collision-safe).

The fold is atomic on guard failure (a malformed row/nested list leaves draft_state entirely untouched) and raises a clear domain error on a malformed prefill (non-list, non-dict row, or a non-list nested segment value).

The folded draft_state round-trips through PlanDraftState and the steps-{i}-{segment}-{k}-{field} keys read back with their values; the whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

tasks.description: Accumulation seam sub-slice 5 (B2 nested-bindings fold, the LAST seam fold): a server-side function in planner/services.py folding the B2 bind-measurement-channels prefill -- list[{criterion_index: int, channel_indices: [int, ...]}] -- into the flat draft_state under the TWO-LEVEL nested key criteria-{criterion_index}-measurement-0-channel-{k}-channel_index (measurement row always 0 -- the blank extra grouping channel bindings per criterion; channel sub-row {k}; field channel_index). Writes only the channel-binding rows (management-form reconstruction for save is the save slice, consistent with the prior folds deferring management forms). criterion_index + channel_index stored as integers as-is (resolved at save). Per-criterion REPLACE scope ^criteria-{i}-measurement-\d+-channel-\d+-[^-]+$ (collision-safe vs the criteria simple-formset fold's ^criteria-\d+-[^-]+$). Carries forward the atomic pre-validation + duplicate-index-guard + unexpected-shape-guard lessons. Addresses FEAT-010 / STORY-013; harness SLICE=fold_b2.
acceptance_criteria: Folding the B2 prefill writes, for each row, criteria-{criterion_index}-measurement-0-channel-{k}-channel_index = channel_indices[k] for each k (the two-level nested key, measurement index 0).
criterion_index and channel_index values are stored as integers unchanged (not resolved at fold time).

REPLACE per criterion: re-folding a criterion's bindings clears its prior criteria-{i}-measurement-{j}-channel-{k}-* rows (incl. stale higher-index) without clobbering the criterion's scalar fields, the measurement row's own fields, the channel/measurement management-form keys, or OTHER criteria's keys.

Folding B2 does not disturb the criteria simple-formset scalars (no collision: the criteria fold's ^criteria-\d+-[^-]+$ scope does not match the deeper measurement/channel keys, and B2's scope does not match criteria scalars).

The fold is atomic on guard failure (a malformed row/nested list leaves draft_state untouched) and raises a clear domain error on a malformed prefill (non-list, non-dict row, non-list channel_indices) or a duplicate criterion_index within the call.

The folded draft_state round-trips through PlanDraftState and the two-level channel-binding keys read back with their integer values; the whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

tasks.description: Trio per-spec server-side loop (FEAT-010 slice 3, HAPPY-PATH only): a function run_trio_pass(school, author, draft_id, seed) in planner/services.py (or planner/orchestration.py) that loops the 14 specs in a fixed dependency order, threading the flat draft_state through the PlanDraftState store. Per spec: load latest draft_state -> build_grounding(school, draft_state, spec.grounding_include) -> fetch+render the PromptTemplate (slug=spec.template_slug; render(**grounding, seed)) -> LLM call via get_llm_logged(author).complete() (full text, logged to LLMCall) -> spec.parse(text, school, grounding) -> spec.resolve (if any) -> route the parsed/resolved prefill to the correct seam fold (the SPEC_FOLD_ROUTING table) -> save_version (append). The order is CONSTRAINED by parse-time draft_state reads: D1 before F1 (F1 reads milestone types specs.py:840); B1+C1 before B2 (B2 reads criteria+feedback counts :1765-66); F1 before F2/F3/F4/F5/US-LLM-27 (read steps count :1941..2652); D1+C1 before G1 (:3805-06). HAPPY-PATH: no retry/degrade (slice #7), no gate-closure (slice #8), no save_nested/promote (slice #9). Reuses AssistStreamView's single-spec sequence (views.py:513-614) non-HTTP + all 6 seam folds. Addresses FEAT-010; harness SLICE=trio_pass.
acceptance_criteria: run_trio_pass runs all 14 specs in a fixed dependency order; for each it builds grounding from the loaded draft_state, renders the spec's template, calls the LLM, parses, resolves, routes the result to the correct seam fold, and appends a new draft_state version.
The spec order satisfies every parse-time draft_state read so no spec's range-check spuriously fails: D1 before F1; B1+C1 before B2; F1 before F2/F3/F4/F5/US-LLM-27; D1+C1 before G1.

Each spec's parsed+resolved prefill is routed to its correct seam fold (form / formset[prefix] / multisection[A3] / review-loop[G1] / per-step-child[segment(s)] / b2-bindings); the final accumulated draft_state contains the folded keys of all 14 specs (spot-checked across the shape classes).
The PlanDraftState versions append per spec (walkable history); the final version holds the full 14-spec accumulation; build_grounding for spec N sees specs 1..N-1's folded output.
LLM calls go through the logged provider (get_llm_logged); tests drive all 14 specs with canned per-spec outputs via FakeLLMProvider (no real API call).

Happy-path scope only: the loop does not retry/degrade on failure, does not run the promotion gate, and does not save a Plan or promote (those are slices 7/8/9); the whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

tasks.description: Trio resilience layer (FEAT-010 slice 4): wrap the per-spec body in run_trio_pass (planner/orchestration.py, the TRIO_SEQUENCE loop calling _run_one_spec + save_version) so a per-spec failure does NOT abort the 14-spec run. Bounded retry with machine-readable feedback, then degrade-to-recorded-entry + continue. Retryable: transient LLM errors (LLMTimeoutError/LLMRateLimitError, retry unchanged) + parse/catalogue ValueError (re-render with the rejection message appended as steering, up to cap R, default 2). Non-retryable (immediate degrade, no retry): LLMAuthenticationError + fold shape errors (TypeError/ValueError from the seam folds -- a parser-bug signal). On exhaustion/non-retryable: record a DegradedSpec (spec_key, attempts, reason, severity=MUST_FIX), skip that spec's fold/save (contributes nothing), continue. run_trio_pass gains a 4th return value: the list of DegradedSpec. Cascade-tolerant (later specs see 0 prior rows gracefully, already true). DegradedSpec is a RETURNED structure (Flag-MODEL-row persistence is slice #9; the gate-closure re-invoke is #8). Realizes DEC-57 dec-4 + DEC-58 + the user's no-aborts requirement. Addresses FEAT-010; harness SLICE=resilience.
acceptance_criteria: A per-spec failure (LLM error, parse/catalogue rejection, or fold error) does NOT abort run_trio_pass; the run completes and returns, with the failing spec recorded rather than raising.

Retryable failures retry up to a bounded cap (default 2): a parse/catalogue ValueError re-renders the spec's prompt with the rejection message appended as steering before the next attempt; a transient LLM error (timeout/rate-limit) retries the same prompt. A spec that succeeds on a retry folds normally and is NOT recorded as degraded.
Non-retryable failures (LLM authentication error; a fold shape error TypeError/ValueError) degrade immediately with no retry.

After exhausting retries (or immediately for non-retryable), the spec degrades to a recorded entry (spec_key, attempt count, reason, severity) and the loop continues; the degraded spec contributes nothing to draft_state.

run_trio_pass returns the list of degraded specs alongside the accumulated draft_state; a degraded prior spec does not crash a later spec (cascade-tolerant -- the later spec sees the missing contribution gracefully).

The LLM calls remain logged; the whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates. (Flag-model-row persistence and the promotion-gate-closure re-invoke are slices #9/#8, out of scope here.)

tasks.description: Trio save layer (FEAT-010 slice 8a-ii, DEC-59): persist the accumulated flat draft_state from run_trio_pass (planner/orchestration.py) into a saved Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED) owned by school+author, with the full child graph. DEPENDS ON TASK-069 (A1 must emit `title` first). The save (1) derives the required Plan.cycle via ai.services.grounding._active_cycle(school) and injects it as the bare `cycle` key (surfacing a precondition failure if the school has no active cycle, never fabricating); (2) synthesizes the formset management forms (TOTAL_FORMS/INITIAL_FORMS at every level: top-level step + extra formsets, and nested criteria-{i}-measurement, criteria-{i}-measurement-{j}-channel, steps-{i}-{seg}, steps-{i}-evidence, review_events-{i}-input) which the folds never write, counting rows by the {prefix}-(d+)- regex; (3) builds a QueryDict (single value per key — collectDraftState/the planner formset M2M model is single-valued) and reuses the PlanCreateView bind->validate->_save_plan(PROPOSED)+save_nested+_save_flag_requests path so the saved graph is identical to the browser one-shot create (parity), atomic all-or-nothing. Out of scope: promotion-gate check, gate-closure re-invoke (8b), admin-promote + Flag persistence (9). Addresses FEAT-010; harness SLICE=save_draft.
acceptance_criteria: The accumulated flat draft_state from run_trio_pass is saved as a Plan in lifecycle=proposed, owned by the originating school and author, with the full graph populated: plan scalars, every formset row, every nested child, and every cross-row binding the draft_state carried.

The save round-trips: re-reading the saved Plan reproduces the draft_state's content (scalar values, formset row counts, nested children, and bindings) with nothing dropped, duplicated, or reordered into a different binding.

For a given draft_state, the saved Plan graph is identical to what the existing browser one-shot create flow persists from the same posted data (parity), and the save is atomic all-or-nothing: a draft_state whose forms/formsets fail validation persists no Plan and surfaces the validation failure rather than a partial or invalid Plan.

The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates. The promotion-gate check, the gate-closure re-invoke loop, admin-promote, and Flag-row persistence are out of scope (slices 8b/9).

tasks.description: Trio save prerequisite (FEAT-010 slice 8a-i, DEC-59): extend the A1 narrative-draft spec to emit a concise plan `title`, so the accumulated trio draft_state carries the Plan.title the save (TASK-068) requires. Mechanism: add `title` to NARRATIVE_FIELDS (planner/specs.py:403) so parse_narrative keeps it; edit the DB PromptTemplate(slug='plan-narrative-draft') body to instruct the model to also produce a concise `title` key, landed via an ai/00NN data migration using the guarded byte-match pattern (ai/0016) so it applies only to the known prior template body. The existing form-step fold already passes bare narrative keys through, so `title` flows to the unprefixed PlanForm.title with no fold change. Title is system-PROPOSED per NORTH-STAR (admin writes only current+desired); a mechanical/placeholder title is a fabrication and is rejected. Out of scope: cycle (TASK-068 derives it via _active_cycle), the save itself (TASK-068). Addresses FEAT-010.
acceptance_criteria: A trio run's accumulated draft_state carries a non-empty `title` (within the Plan.title 200-char limit) that binds to PlanForm.title, produced by A1's reasoning from its grounding rather than a fixed or placeholder string.

A1's existing four narrative scalars (current_state, desired_state, rationale, student_impact_framing) still parse and fold unchanged; an A1 response lacking a title degrades gracefully to a partial prefill exactly as a missing scalar does today.
The production prompt-template change applies only to the expected prior template body (a guarded change, not a blind overwrite) and is reversible.
The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

tasks.description: Trio gate-closure loop (FEAT-010 slice 8b, US-LLM-25). Build run_gate_closure_loop in planner/orchestration.py: given the trio's accumulated draft_state (school, author), iterate to a draft that PASSES plans/services.py _check_promotion_gates. Each round: probe = save the draft_state to a Plan via save_draft_state_as_plan inside a savepoint/atomic that ROLLS BACK (save is create-only, so probing-then-rollback avoids orphan Plans), run _check_promotion_gates(plan), collect the unmet predicate messages; map each unmet predicate -> the producing spec and re-invoke it (single-spec _run_one_spec against the CURRENT draft_state, then re-fold -- folds are REPLACE-scoped so a re-invoke replaces that spec's rows); re-probe. Bounded by a hard max-rounds cap. On convergence: one real (committed) save -> the gate-passing Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED). On cap-exhaustion: return the residual unmet predicates (no abort, no raise). Continue/stop is the DETERMINISTIC gate (DEC-58 permits the bounded autonomous loop). Gate-failure->spec mapping (derivable from TRIO_SEQUENCE + the gate predicates, verified vs source): plan scalars title/current_state/desired_state/rationale/student_impact_framing -> A1; learner_outcomes/areas_for_improvement/stakeholder_impact -> A3; accreditation_standards -> A2; success criteria -> B1; criterion measurement-channel binding -> B2 (needs C1 feedback channels); action steps -> F1; step responsible/timeline/resource/evidence -> F2/F3/F4/F5; milestone planning_method -> D1; communications + review_events + inputs + revision_rules -> G1 (G1 produces ALL of these). RESIDUAL predicates no spec closes: cycle (the save derives it via _active_cycle; absent active cycle = a precondition failure, surfaced not retried); the Phase-14 plan.clean() priority_rationale-on-deviation rule + unresolved MUST_FIX flags (not produced/triggered by normal trio output) -> surfaced as residual. SCOPE: does NOT flip lifecycle (admin-only, advance_plan_status) and does NOT persist Flag rows -- those are slice #9. Addresses FEAT-010; harness SLICE=gate_closure.
acceptance_criteria: A draft_state that initially fails one or more spec-closable promotion-gate predicates is brought, by the loop, to a saved Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED) that PASSES _check_promotion_gates (the unmet predicates' producing specs are re-invoked and their output re-folded until the gate holds).

The loop terminates deterministically: it is bounded by a hard max-rounds cap, and on cap-exhaustion with the gate still failing it returns the residual unmet predicate messages without aborting, raising, or looping unbounded.

An unmet predicate that no trio spec can close (e.g. no active cycle, or a Phase-14 clean()/flag condition) is reported as a residual rather than retried; a spec-closable predicate is not misreported as residual.
The iteration leaves no orphan or partial Plan rows (probe rounds persist nothing); the loop does NOT change plan.lifecycle and does NOT write any Flag rows (slice #9 scope).
The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

tasks.description: Trio final-save + degraded-spec Flag persistence + promote verification (FEAT-010 slice #9). Compose the production trio into one orchestration entry in planner/orchestration.py: run_trio_pass -> run_gate_closure_loop -> persist the still-degraded specs as MUST_FIX Flag rows on the saved Plan -> return (Plan, residual_failures). Each DegradedSpec (orchestration.py; spec_key/attempts/reason/severity=FlagSeverity.MUST_FIX) that REMAINS unproduced after the closure loop (its section absent from the final draft_state -- a degraded spec the closure loop did NOT subsequently produce) becomes one plans.models.flag.Flag(plan, element_path=spec:<key>, message=reason, created_by_spec=<key>, verdict=None, severity=MUST_FIX, is_resolved=False); a spec that succeeded initially OR was recovered by a closure re-invoke gets NO flag. The composition ALWAYS yields a saved Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED): the closure loop's gate-passing Plan when it converges, else the best-effort draft saved anyway (so the admin always gets an editable proposed draft), carrying its MUST_FIX flags; residual gate failures returned to the caller. ADMIN-PROMOTE NEEDS NO NEW WIRING (verified): plans/admin/actions.py:advance_plan_status_action (registered on PlanAdmin) drives plans/services.advance_plan_status, which re-runs _check_promotion_gates on the draft->active transition, and the gate already blocks on plan.flags.filter(severity=MUST_FIX, is_resolved=False) (services.py:203-204). The AI NEVER flips lifecycle -- the admin alone promotes. SCOPE: persist degraded-spec Flags + the composition + verify the promote gate enforces them; does NOT add a user-facing trigger to START a trio run (no view/button/command -- that is a separate entry-point slice) and does NOT modify advance_plan_status/_check_promotion_gates/run_gate_closure_loop. Addresses FEAT-010; harness SLICE=trio_finalize.
acceptance_criteria: After a trio run, each spec that remains degraded -- its section unproduced in the final post-closure draft_state -- is persisted as exactly one MUST_FIX Flag on the saved Plan (carrying the failing spec key and its reason); a spec whose section is present, whether produced in the initial sweep or recovered by a closure-loop re-invocation, produces NO flag.
A clean trio run (no spec remains degraded) persists ZERO must-fix flags on the saved Plan.

The composition always yields a saved Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED): when the closure loop converges, that gate-passing Plan; when it cannot, the best-effort draft is still saved as a proposed Plan carrying its must-fix flags, and the residual gate failures are returned to the caller -- the run never dead-ends without an editable draft and never aborts.

An unresolved must-fix flag on the saved Plan blocks promotion through the existing admin path (advance_plan_status into ACTIVE raises on the gate); resolving that flag on an otherwise gate-passing Plan lets promotion proceed; nothing in the trio path flips lifecycle itself.
Re-running the composition does not double-persist flags for the same degradation; the whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

tasks.description: Minimal trigger for the production trio (FEAT-010): a Django management command planner/management/commands/run_trio.py that invokes the existing planner.orchestration.run_trio_to_proposed_plan(school, author, draft_id, seed, max_rounds) -> (Plan, residual) and reports the result. Mirrors the house command pattern (school/management/commands/import_responsibility_atoms.py): add_arguments (--school-slug default chiway-repton-xiamen resolving School.objects.get(slug=...); --author-email resolving the User to stamp plan.author, falling back to a superuser; --seed; --max-rounds; --dry-run) + handle (resolve school+author with CommandError on failure; mint draft_id=uuid4; run inside transaction.atomic with set_rollback on dry-run; catch the no-active-cycle ValidationError -> CommandError; report via self.stdout/self.style the Plan pk/title/lifecycle, the residual gate-failure messages, and the MUST_FIX plan.flags). Create the planner/management + commands package __init__.py (planner has no management dir). THIN trigger only -- no new orchestration/gate/promote/Flag logic, no lifecycle flip (admin alone promotes via the existing advance_plan_status_action), no view/admin action (the streaming planner view is a separate later slice). Realizes the trio's user-invocability; unblocks the post-Phase-15 end-to-end walkthrough + the US-LLM-23/24/25 vivified flips. Addresses FEAT-010; harness SLICE=trio_command.
acceptance_criteria: Invoking the command for a school that has an active cycle, with an author, runs the trio end-to-end and persists a Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED) owned by that school and author; the command reports the saved Plan's identity (pk/title/lifecycle), the residual gate-failure messages, and the persisted MUST_FIX flags.

A precondition failure -- a missing/unknown school, an unresolvable author, or a school with no active cycle -- exits with a clear CommandError and persists NO Plan (never a raw traceback, never a partial Plan).
--dry-run runs the trio but persists no Plan (the work is rolled back); the command still reports what it would have produced.

The command is a thin trigger: it adds no orchestration/gate/promote/Flag logic of its own (it invokes the existing run_trio_to_proposed_plan and reads back the returned Plan/residual), does not flip plan.lifecycle, adds no view/admin action, and modifies none of the trio internals.
The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

tasks.description: Fix FGAP-050: thread the run's user through the parse->free-text-audit->fabrication-validator chain so the DEC-44 validator (planner/_llm_fabrication_validator.py:validate_candidates) resolves the LLM via get_llm(user=user) -- the SAME django-configured provider the trio's spec calls use (get_llm_logged(author)) -- instead of get_llm() with no user falling through to an unconfigured default and silently no-opping. Add an optional user=None through: validate_candidates (get_llm(user=user)); _freetext_audit.py audit_freetext_flags(849)/audit_freetext(838)/audit_prefill_flags(1368) (forward to the validate_candidates call at 1014); the 12 specs.py parsers that call audit_prefill_flags (+ user=None on the remaining parsers for spec.parse signature compat); the two entry points spec.parse(...) at orchestration.py:270 (user=author) and views.py:619 (user=request.user). Make the cannot-run case FAIL LOUD: in validate_candidates distinguish NoProviderConfiguredError (re-raise -- the backstop genuinely cannot run) from transient/parse errors (keep WARNING + return [] graceful). user defaults None (backward-compatible). No change to get_llm semantics, the resilience loop, or the deterministic free-text audit. Addresses FEAT-010 + FGAP-050; harness SLICE=fabrication_validator.
acceptance_criteria: The DEC-44 fabrication validator resolves its LLM via the run's user, so under the normal user-scoped provider config it runs against the SAME provider the trio's spec calls use -- no fall-through to an unconfigured default, no silent no-op.

A genuine no-provider / cannot-run failure SURFACES (is not swallowed as an empty 'no fabrications found' result); a transient provider error (timeout/rate-limit/network) or a malformed-response parse failure still degrades gracefully (returns no fabricated spans) exactly as today.

Both entry paths thread their user -- the trio run (_run_one_spec's author) and the browser AssistStreamView (request.user); a caller that omits the user keeps today's behavior (backward-compatible, user=None default).
The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

tasks.description: FGAP-051 step 1 (user-directed, empirical-first): stop an over-long LLM label/name/title from ABORTING the trio run, via the PROMPT + the existing bounded retry -- NO truncation, model field caps kept (not sacred; relaxing is the contingent step-2 fallback only if the dry-run shows the LLM still overflows). (a) PROMPT: a guarded ai/00NN migration adds to the ~5 capped-field-producing spec templates (A1 narrative-draft title; C1 suggest-feedback-channels label+instrument; D1 propose-milestones label; F5 propose-evidence label; G1 propose-review-loop review-event label + communication channel) a concise, communicative directive naming the REASON -- the value is stored in a 200-char (instrument 500) database column and will fail to save if longer, so keep it concise + communicative, well under the limit. (b) RETRY: the capped-field parsers (planner/specs.py) validate length after the non-empty check -> raise ValueError on overflow (message names the field, its length, and the cap) -> the EXISTING resilience loop (_run_spec_with_resilience, DEFAULT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=2 = 1 initial + 1 retry) re-renders with the rejection as steering. Bounded, not wasteful. On retry-exhaustion the existing per-spec degrade applies (the over-long value never reaches the terminal save), so the run COMPLETES rather than aborting -- a strict improvement over the current whole-run abort; NO truncation. Then DRY-RUN run_trio and observe whether the prompt+retry makes the LLM comply (FGAP-051 stays open until the real dry-run confirms no abort). Addresses FEAT-010 + FGAP-051; harness SLICE=label_length.
acceptance_criteria: The capped-field-producing specs' prompts instruct the LLM, with the reason (the value is stored in a fixed-length DB column and will break on save if longer), to produce a concise, communicative value within the column limit.

An over-long value from a capped-field spec triggers ONE bounded retry-with-feedback (the rejection naming the field, its length, and the cap) via the existing resilience mechanism -- not wasteful, and with NO truncation of the value.

An over-long value no longer ABORTS the run: the length validation + retry keep the over-long value out of the terminal save, so the run completes and yields a saved proposed Plan (worst case, on retry-exhaustion, the offending spec degrades per the existing resilience -- the run still completes; the value is never truncated and the model field caps are unchanged).
The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates; the prompt-template migration applies forward and reverses cleanly (guarded byte-match).
